channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Govee’s solar-powered string lights are already on sale for 20 percent off
item.description: I’m not sure if we’ve mentioned this yet, but Govee has been on a tear lately, having recently announced everything from rechargeable table lamps to multicolor, Matter-compatible ceiling lights that can display custom images with retro, pixelated flair. In April, the company also unveiled a 34-foot set of Outdoor Solar String Lights, which are now […]...

item.title: Truecaller slashes 70 jobs amid declining ad sales
item.description: Truecaller's ad revenue declined by 44%, leading to the layoffs.

item.title: Why AI auditability is what every security leader should be talking about
item.description: There was a time when "audit" meant conducting a periodic look back at what happened. That changed with digital transformation.

item.title: The future of game consoles is looking bleak
item.description: It's been a real good news / bad news week for Nintendo. Out of nowhere on Wednesday, the company announced a lush remake of Star Fox 64, jolting the dormant franchise to life and helping to fill out a relatively sparse lineup for the Switch 2 for the rest of the year. But then on […]

item.title: Live only at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Why most founders are already behind on raising a Series A in 2027
item.description: Learn what it takes to build a Series A in 2027 from top VCs at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, taking place at San Francisco’s Moscone West on October 13-15. Register today to hear this session live on the Builders Stage.

item.title: OpenAI rolls out new model for cybersecurity teams a month after Anthropic's Mythos debut
item.description: GPT-5.5-Cyber is now available to TAC members, but it's a slight upgrade from 5.4.

item.title: Ever wish for earbuds with the disc-style fit of Apple's original iPod buds, but with hi-fi-quality sound and a USB-C cable? Then I've got great news for you — but don't expect them to come in all-white
item.description: The Sivga M260 look like Apple's iPod earbuds, but with a big driver, premium build and multiple connection options.

item.title: Boox’s new page-turning e-reader remote is a tiny two-button keyboard
item.description: Boox has announced its own alternative to the Kobo Remote that offers more functionality than just turning the page while reading on its tablets and e-readers. The Tappy can also be used to scroll vertical content in browsers or social media apps and skip to the next or previous track when listening to music or […]

item.title: What’s the role of a simple fitness band in the AI health era?
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent every Friday from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. Opt in for Optimizer here. A fitness band is for bettering yourself, but casually. It's lightweight, easy to wear, and not something you […]

item.title: Last 24 hours to get 50% off a second pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.description: Last day to buy one pass and get a second one at 50% off to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Bring a partner, co-founder, or colleague at half off. Register now.

item.title: 7 new movies and TV shows to watch on Netflix, Prime Video, HBO Max, and more this weekend (May 8)
item.description: From another Lord of the Flies remake to three great-looking new movies, here's what you'll want to watch this weekend.

item.title: Boox announces Tappy, a wireless page-turning remote
item.description: Tappy can work with other Bluetooth devices, too.

item.title: Courbe de réponse et égaliseur : le guide pour comprendre et corriger le son de vos écouteurs et enceintes
item.description: La courbe de réponse d'un écouteur révèle en un coup d'œil ce que des heures d'écoute peinent à formuler. Trop de graves, médiums creux, aigus agressifs : tout est là, lisible, et surtout corrigeable avec l'égaliseur de votre application.

item.title: Engadget Podcast: Gamestop's wild eBay gamble
item.description: And we review the reMarkable Paper Pure.

item.title: Sony and Canon are unveiling new cameras on the same day next week — and I know which one I’m more excited about
item.description: Sony has shared a teaser on it YouTube channel of a new 'R' camera – releasing the same week as a mysterious new Canon camera.

item.title: Meta acquires Moltbook but is our data safe?
item.description: Meta's acquisition of AI-agent social network Moltbook raises fresh data privacy concerns for billions of users.

item.title: 'Why does technology just keep getting less fun?' Fitbit users are mad about losing key features as a result of the huge Google Health changes — but I want to hear what you think
item.description: Fitbit's gone from a straight wellness hub to an AI subscription service.

item.title: Tesla is recalling its cheaper Cybertruck because the wheels might fall off
item.description: Tesla is recalling its RWD Cybertruck Long Range over faulty brake rotors that could cause the wheels to fall off, as spotted earlier by MotorTrend. In a notice on the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration's website, Tesla says "brake rotor stud holes may crack and allow the stud to separate from the wheel hub." The […]

item.title: Dacia prépare la suite de la Spring : une Twingo électrique en moins cher ?
item.description: La remplaçante de la Spring arrive prochainement. Possiblement basée sur la plateforme de la Renault Twingo, cette citadine, qui pourrait s’appeler Evader, deviendra la voiture électrique la moins chère du marché.

item.title: Everybody wants to rule the AI world
item.description: Sometimes, companies pick CEOs based on carefully laid succession plans designed to maximize investor confidence and future performance. Other times, apparently, companies pick CEOs based on a bunch of video calls while the current CEO is texting the former CEO about who the new CEO even is. Such was the story of The Blip, the […]

item.title: 7 mistakes your business could make when choosing a VoIP system
item.description: Switching to VoIP can cut costs and improve how your team communicates, but only if you choose the right system. Here are seven common pitfalls to avoid before you commit.

item.title: Groks voice mode comes to Apple CarPlay
item.description: SpaceXAI's smart assistant Grok is now available in CarPlay.

item.title: Lime, the Uber-backed micromobility company, files for IPO
item.description: After years of hints and preparation, Lime plans to enter the public markets.

item.title: Cette lampe Philips Hue avec son large éventail de couleurs éteint 30 euros de son prix Amazon
item.description: Nomade et personnalisable à l'infini, la Philips Hue Go Portable est une superbe lampe pour ceux qui souhaitent adapter la luminosité de leur intérieur selon les situations. En ce moment, elle est disponible avec une réduction de 33 % chez Amazon.

item.title: How to watch Deadliest Catch season 22 online — stream the hunt for king crab from anywhere, free trial
item.description: A major fleet relocation, a tougher stretch of water and a season shaped by real loss. Here's how to watch Deadliest Catch season 22 online, including its US premiere details and the latest on international availability.

item.title: Blackmagic Camera hands-on: Apple Watch compatibility gives vloggers remote control
item.description: Capture high-quality Apple ProRes video with full stabilization.

item.title: Tech layoffs this week: Cloudflare, Coinbase, Upwork, and others point to AI as they slash jobs
item.description: In the drive to become “AI native,” more companies this week said they will significantly reduce the size of their workforces. April was not a good month for the tech industry in terms of job losses. Last month, major firms—including Microsoft, Meta Platforms, and Snap—all announced significant workforce reductions.

item.title: The biggest U.S. power grid is under strain from AI — and no one is happy
item.description: PJM Interconnection — which oversees the grid for some of the densest data center developments on Earth — wants to overhaul itself. Not everyone thinks it's up to the task.

item.title: Disney CEO Josh D’Amaro wants to merge Disney+ into a ‘super app’, but it’s not as thrilling as you’d think — instead you’ll be forced to see ads pushing theme parks and cruise lines, and who really wants that?
item.description: Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro wants to merge Disney+ with Disneyland Resort and Disney Cruise Line Navigator apps.

item.title: 5% GPU utilization: The $401 billion AI infrastructure problem enterprises can't keep ignoring
item.description: For the last 24 months, one narrative justified every over-provisioned data center and bloated IT budget: the GPU scramble. Silicon was the new oil, and H100s traded like contraband. Reserve capacity now or your enterprise would be left behind.The bill is now due, and the CFO is paying attention. Gartner estimates AI infrastructure is adding $401 billion in new spending this year. Real-world audits tell a darker story: average GPU utilization in the enterprise is stuck at 5%. That utilization floor is driven by a self-reinforcing procurement loop that makes idle GPUs nearly impossible to relea...

item.title: Microsoft is fixing one of the worst problems with Windows 11 updates — those dreaded installation failures
item.description: Some Windows 11 updates might be slower in the future — but it's for a good reason.
